title: session-storage-db SQLite schema (v1)
description: Per-session SQLite database schema. One file per session at <storage-root>/session/<sid>.db. Tables: messages, parts, meta. WAL journal mode + synchronous=NORMAL.

MessagesTable.description: One row per message. id is the canonical identifier (msg_xxxxxxx). Insertion order = id order (ascending).

ddl.const: CREATE TABLE messages (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL, role TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (role IN ('user','assistant')), parent_id TEXT, time_created INTEGER NOT NULL, time_completed INTEGER, model_id TEXT, provider_id TEXT, account_id TEXT, mode TEXT, agent TEXT, finish TEXT, tokens_input INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, tokens_output INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, tokens_total INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, tokens_cache_read INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, tokens_cache_write INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, tokens_reasoning INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, cost REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, summary INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, error_json TEXT, info_extra_json TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}')

default: CREATE INDEX idx_messages_role_id ON messages(role, id)
CREATE INDEX idx_messages_summary ON messages(summary) WHERE summary = 1

parent_id.description: for assistant messages, the user message id they reply to

tokens_total.description: DD-6: read directly by filterCompacted, replaces JSON.stringify estimation

info_extra_json.description: JSON-encoded fallback for fields that don't fit promoted columns; round-trips MessageV2.Info shape losslessly

PartsTable.description: One row per part. Sequence + id together preserve in-order render even if ids are non-monotonic across part types.

ddl.const: CREATE TABLE parts (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL, message_id TEXT NOT NULL, sequence INTEGER NOT NULL, type TEXT NOT NULL, payload_json TEXT NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (message_id) REFERENCES messages(id) ON DELETE CASCADE)

default: CREATE INDEX idx_parts_message ON parts(message_id, sequence)

payload_json.description: full part body as JSON; lossless round-trip of MessageV2.Part shape

MetaTable.description: Per-session key-value metadata. Schema version + migration history live here.

ddl.const: CREATE TABLE meta (key TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL, value TEXT NOT NULL)

schema_version.description: integer as string; matches DD-7 / DR-5 user_version semantics

session_id.description: redundant safety check; must equal filename minus .db

migrated_from_legacy.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when dreaming-mode migration completed; absent for fresh-born SQLite sessions

legacy_message_count.description: row count assertion captured at migration-time for DR-4 verification

schema_version.description: Initial release. Bumped via DD-7 / DR-5 migration scripts.

pragmas.description: PRAGMAs applied at every connection open.

default: messages.parent_id when present must be present in messages.id (FK enforced via app layer; not declared in DDL because role='user' messages may have no row referencing them yet at write time)
parts.sequence is unique per message_id (enforced by application layer; not by DDL to allow streaming append before final ordering)
tokens_total := tokens_input + tokens_output + tokens_cache_read + tokens_cache_write (or computed equivalently); enforced at write time
